statement_lock.original_statement:
Let $f(n)$ be the maximum number of mutually orthogonal Latin squares of order $n$. Is it true that\[f(n) \gg n^{1/2}?\]




Euler conjectured that $f(n)=1$ when $n\equiv 2\pmod{4}$, but this was disproved by Bose, Parker, and Shrikhande \cite{BPS60} who proved $f(n)\geq 2$ for $n\geq 7$. Chowla, Erd\H{o}s, and Straus \cite{CES60} proved $f(n) \gg n^{1/91}$. Wilson \cite{Wi74} proved $f(n) \gg n^{1/17}$. Beth \cite{Be83c} proved $f(n) \gg n^{1/14.8}$. The sequence of $f(n)$ is A001438 in the OEIS.




References

[BPS60] Bose, R. C. and Shrikhande, S. S. and Parker, E. T., Further results on the construction of mutually orthogonal Latin squares and the falsity of Euler's conjecture . Canadian J. Math. (1960), 189-203.

[Be83c] Beth, Thomas, Eine Bemerkung zur Absch\"{a}tzung der Anzahl orthogonaler lateinischer Quadrate mittels Siebverfahren . Abh. Math. Sem. Univ. Hamburg (1983), 284-288.

[CES60] Chowla, S. and Erd\H{o}s, P. and Straus, E. G., On the maximal number of pairwise orthogonal Latin squares of a given order . Canadian J. Math. (1960), 204-208.

[Wi74] Wilson, Richard M., Concerning the number of mutually orthogonal Latin squares . Discrete Math. (1974), 181-198.
acceptance_criteria: Prove or disprove the exact original statement without changing quantifiers.
Do not add hypotheses, restrict domains, weaken conclusions, or omit subproblems.
Resolve every boundary case and every explicitly numbered task.
Treat ambiguity as a blocking defect rather than choosing a convenient interpretation.

subgoal_graph.summary:
The immutable statement is not presently adjudicable because the symbol ≫ has no supplied definition; GATE0 is therefore an external specification gate, not a mathematical lemma. Conditional on an external definition as the eventual absolute-constant assertion, the primary constructive branch is a critical-scale PBD decomposition using four prime-power block sizes. The secondary constructive branch is a dyadic-window incomplete transversal-design construction. Direct orthogonal-array hypergraph matching is retained as a fallback, and a universal compatible-resolution bound at orders 2p is retained as the disproof branch. Computation is optional and may only support claims backed by explicit certificates. No terminal branch is complete, so no solution is claimed. Source artifact: 

Pasted text

subgoals.claim: Obtain an external specification of the quantifiers and constant dependence encoded by ≫. Until this is supplied, the exact immutable statement remains formally ambiguous and cannot be proved or disproved under the acceptance criteria.

Conditional target declaration only: if GATE0 defines f(n) ≫ n^(1/2) to mean that there exist absolute constants c>0 and N such that f(n)≥c sqrt(n) for every integer n≥N, denote that quantified assertion by Q.

Prove directly that k MOLS of order n are equivalent to each of the following: an OA(n^2,k+2,n,2); n^2 words of length k+2 over an n-symbol alphabet with pairwise agreement in at most one coordinate; and a transversal design with k+2 groups of size n.

Using the orthogonal-partition subspaces over the real field, prove f(n)≤n−1. Explicitly use the finite-dimensional facts dim(U+W)=dim U+dim W for orthogonal subspaces, rank-nullity over R, and that the orthogonal complement of the all-ones vector in R^(n^2) has dimension n^2−1.

Prove the prime-power and idempotent ingredients: for every prime power q, construct q−1 MOLS of order q; define g(n) as the maximum number of idempotent MOLS; prove f(n)−1≤g(n)≤f(n); and construct at least q−2 idempotent MOLS of order q.
Prove PBD closure: if every block of a pairwise balanced design on v points supports t idempotent MOLS, then the whole point set supports t idempotent MOLS.

Prove the critical PBD barrier. For a nontrivial finite PBD, let A be its real point-block incidence matrix. State and use rank(A)=rank(A^T) and dim ker(A^T)=v−rank(A) for finite real matrices to prove that A has full row rank and hence at least v blocks. Deduce that if every block supports k idempotent MOLS, then (k+1)(k+2)≤v−1.

For each integer x≥2, let q be the least power of 2 at least x, let r be the least power of 3 at least x, and set K_x={q,2q,r,3r}. Verify that every member is a prime power in [x,9x], that gcd{k−1:k in K_x}=1, and that gcd{k(k−1):k in K_x}=2, so the elementary PBD divisibility conditions impose no residue restriction on the order v.
Prove the conditional reduction: if an absolute C exists such that every sufficiently large x and every v≥C x^2 admit a PBD(v,K_x), then Q follows from F3 and F4 by taking x=floor(sqrt(v/C)).

Derive all exact local and global feasibility constraints for PBD(v,K_x), including point-degree equations, pair counting, the real-incidence Fisher bound, parity coverage by even blocks, and the block-size restrictions implied by F5. Use these constraints to determine admissible ranges for the constant C without treating them as sufficient.

Primary constructive task: for some absolute C, construct for every sufficiently large x and every v≥C x^2 an approximate decomposition of K_v into cliques whose orders lie in K_x, leaving an explicitly characterized admissible residual graph of size small enough for a uniform completion mechanism.

Construct a uniform family of trades or absorbers, using only clique sizes from K_x, that exactly decomposes every residual graph allowed by P4 while preserving all pair multiplicities and point-degree congruences.
Primary terminal branch: combine P4 and P5 to prove PBD(v,K_x) for every sufficiently large x and every v≥C x^2; then invoke P2 to establish Q. This branch is currently incomplete.

Define ITD(r,n;q) as an OA(n^2,r,n,2) containing an OA(q^2,r,q,2) on designated q-symbol subalphabets. Prove that ITD(q+1,n;q) with n>q necessarily satisfies n≥q^2 by counting outside rows according to the number of old symbols.

For every prime power q, construct ITD(q+1,q^2;q) using a two-dimensional vector space V over F_q: index coordinates by F_q union {infinity}, set X_t=x+t y and X_infinity=y for x,y in V, and obtain the core by restricting x and y to a fixed one-dimensional F_q-subspace.

Prove the anchored-matching-frame reformulation for ITD(q+1,q^2+t;q): the one-old rows define compatible perfect matchings among the new-symbol groups, and the unused graph between every pair of groups is t-regular; completing the ITD is equivalent to decomposing the residual multipartite graph into transversal K_(q+1) copies.

Secondary branch information-gain task: prove or disprove uniformly for powers of 2 q the first perturbation ITD(q+1,q^2+1;q), where the all-new rows are forced to form exactly one perfect matching. Isolated constructions for fixed q do not discharge this subgoal.

Construct ITD(q+1,n;q) uniformly for every sufficiently large power of 2 q and every integer n with q^2≤n<4q^2, by extending the matching-frame and residual-decomposition mechanisms beyond the first perturbation.

Secondary constructive terminal branch: if I5 is proved, then for each large n choose the largest power of 2 q≤sqrt(n); since q^2≤n<4q^2 and q>sqrt(n)/2, the resulting ITD gives at least q−1 MOLS and establishes Q. This branch is currently incomplete.

Define the exact orthogonal-array hypergraph H_(r,n): vertices are (i,j,a,b) with 1≤i<j≤r and a,b in [n], and for x in [n]^r set e_x={(i,j,x_i,x_j):1≤i<j≤r}. Prove that perfect matchings are exactly OA(n^2,r,n,2) and contain exactly n^2 edges. Each vertex has degree n^(r−2). For r≥3, distinct compatible vertices have codegree at most n^(r−3), while incompatible vertices have codegree zero. For r=2, distinct vertices have codegree zero; when no distinct vertex pair exists, define the maximum over the empty set to be zero.
Prove that H_(r,n) has an exact fractional perfect matching by assigning weight n^(2−r) to every edge, and record precisely why this fractional solution alone gives no integral orthogonal array.

Fallback constructive task: prove an integral perfect-matching theorem for H_(r,n) when r=floor(c sqrt(n))+2 for some absolute c>0, using a fully specified structured-nibble, exchange, or absorption argument that handles edge size Theta(n), exact coverage, and the non-negligible overlap among constraints.
Direct terminal branch: a proof of D3 for all sufficiently large n would produce floor(c sqrt(n)) MOLS and establish Q. This branch is currently incomplete.

Prove the compatible-resolution formulation: for a Latin square L, let rho(L) be the maximum number of pairwise orthogonal resolutions of its cells into transversals; then for n>1, f(n)=1+max_L rho(L). Separately record that product, cyclic-linear, group-table, and quotient-compatible difference constructions fail on many orders 2p, without treating these restricted failures as upper bounds on arbitrary MOLS.

Develop an invariant applying to every Latin square of order 2p, not merely algebraic or translation-based squares, that bounds the number of mutually compatible transversal resolutions. Test whether incidence parity, modular rank, Smith normal form, or intersection constraints can be converted into such a universal bound.

Negative terminal theorem target: prove for an infinite sequence of odd primes p that max_L rho(L)=o(sqrt(p)) over all Latin squares L of order 2p. By N1 this would give f(2p)=o(sqrt(2p)) and disprove Q.

Disproof terminal branch: complete N3 or an equivalent universal infinite-family upper bound. Restricted construction obstructions and finite examples are insufficient. This branch is currently incomplete.

Optional falsification suite: implement certificate-producing exact-cover, SAT, or ILP searches for proposed PBD constants and palettes, first-perturbation ITDs, and small compatible-resolution bounds. A computational result may support only the precise finite claim certified by the output and is not a dependency of a terminal theorem proved independently.

Disjunctive terminal gate: this node is complete when any one, not all, of T_PBD, T_ITD, T_DIRECT, or T_NEG is complete. The listed dependencies are alternative terminal routes rather than conjunctive obligations.

After GATE0 supplies an exact definition, prove or disprove the resulting exact quantified assertion by completing one branch of TERM_OR, with every boundary case and imported theorem hypothesis checked. At present neither the immutable statement nor the conditional target Q has been resolved.

regulator.rationale: The candidate rests on a fundamentally impermissible interpretation: it assigns a meaning to the undefined symbol ≫ even though the immutable-statement lock and acceptance criteria require ambiguity to remain a blocking defect. Because the statement cannot be clarified or changed, neither a local proof repair nor a revised constructive dependency plan can yield an accepted proof or disproof. Independently, every terminal mathematical route remains open, C16 is false under its stated indexing, C12 uses an unstated field-extension theorem, C14 omits the required core hypothesis, C17 fails at r=2, and the GOAL node encodes a textual disjunction as conjunctive dependencies. The correct recovery is therefore to rewrite the response as a formal non-adjudicability diagnosis rather than continue any proof branch under an adopted interpretation.
new_constraints: Do not assign any interpretation to ≫ or derive conclusions conditional on a preferred convention.
State that the exact immutable assertion has no determinate truth condition under the supplied syntax and therefore cannot be proved or disproved under the acceptance criteria.
Do not present the PBD, ITD, hypergraph, or compatible-resolution portfolios as progress toward an accepted terminal resolution unless a genuinely new theorem closes one complete route.
Delete C16 unless its matrix, indexing convention, and all difference multisets are replaced and mechanically verified.
Any replacement for C12 must explicitly state and prove or declare the finite-field extension and subfield theorem used.
Any replacement for C14 must include existence of the order-q core as an explicit hypothesis and verify that the outside construction combines with it.
Restrict the hypergraph codegree formula to a precisely stated domain, including r≥3 and n≥1, and handle smaller boundaries separately.
Define integer thresholds such as N(t) using an attained minimum or an explicit eventual-set convention, with all rounding steps checked.
Represent the final resolution gate as an actual disjunction rather than listing all terminal branches as simultaneous dependencies.
Do not claim that computations, finite certificates, imports, or claim checks were completed unless the corresponding evidence is explicitly supplied and verified.

regulator.rationale:
The defects are structural, not locally repairable. S0 asks the proof to assign an exact quantified meaning to an undefined symbol while the statement lock forbids supplementation; therefore S0 is impossible under the governing rules, and every node depending on its discharge is blocked. The GOAL node also encodes all terminal routes conjunctively although its claim declares them disjunctive, and it incorrectly makes the computational suite mandatory for a theorem proved by an independent terminal route. In addition, the candidate completes no terminal construction or upper-bound route; isolated q=3 and q=4 ITD examples, sparse-palette exclusions, and a fractional hypergraph matching cannot be patched into an asymptotic resolution. R19 has local notation and counting defects, but repairing them would not cure the broken dependency structure. The cheapest valid recovery is therefore to rebuild the plan around an explicit specification gate and, only conditionally on an externally supplied interpretation, select one terminal mathematical branch rather than preserving the impossible S0 dependency. This diagnosis is based solely on the supplied artifact. 

Pasted text
new_constraints: Do not represent S0 as a mathematical subgoal that can be proved from the immutable text. Record it as an external specification gate: under the ambiguity rule, the exact locked assertion is not adjudicable without an externally supplied definition of ≫.
Keep all analysis of the eventual absolute-constant assertion explicitly conditional; it must not be presented as proving or disproving the immutable statement.

Remove S0 as a dependency of purely combinatorial formulations. In particular, S13 may depend on S1 and on a separately declared conditional target parameter r=c√n, but not on a falsely discharged S2.
Encode the terminal resolution as an OR-dependency: completion of S8, or a sufficient ITD theorem from S11-S12, or S14, or S17. Do not require all four branches simultaneously.
Do not make S18 mandatory when a terminal theorem is proved without computation. Require S18 only for claims whose justification relies on computational searches or finite classifications.
Choose one positive terminal branch for primary development. Treat the remaining branches as falsification or fallback routes until a genuinely new theorem-level ingredient appears.
Do not infer asymptotic conclusions from the isolated q=3 and q=4 ITD constructions, finite PBD obstructions, or restricted Latin-square parity obstructions.

Rewrite R19 as e_x={(i,j,x_i,x_j):1≤i<j≤r}; state that every perfect matching has exactly n² edges; delete the unsupported n²/binom(r,2) fragment; and handle the r=2,n=1 empty-codegree convention explicitly.
State every imported linear-algebra fact and its hypotheses, including the scalar field and the rank-nullity implication used in the PBD incidence argument.

Do not report RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED or high completeness scores merely because several reductions were checked. Such a status requires documented exhaustion of the selected approach family, while theorem-level completeness remains zero until a terminal route is finished.

obstructions: Even under the candidate's adopted absolute interpretation, no proof or disproof of f(n) ≥ c√n for all sufficiently large n is obtained.
No exact perfect-matching or absorption theorem is proved for the orthogonal-array hypergraph when r=Θ(√n).
No uniform ITD(q+1,n;q) construction covering the range needed for the asymptotic assertion is proved.
No uniform critical-scale PBD theorem with prime-power block sizes of order Θ(√v) is proved.
No universal infinite-family upper bound on compatible transversal resolutions is proved that would disprove the square-root assertion.
The advertised q=4 first-perturbation construction C16 is invalid as written and requires replacement or repair.
The codegree assertion in C17 requires additional boundary hypotheses, at least r≥3.

The immutable statement does not define the symbol ≫ or its constant dependence. Under the acceptance criteria, this ambiguity is blocking and cannot be removed by adopting a preferred interpretation.

obstructions: For r=2 and n=1, R19's statement that the maximum codegree is zero requires an explicit convention for the maximum of an empty set.
No disjunctive terminal route in the validated subgoal graph is completed.
No exact absorption, structured-nibble, or other integral perfect-matching theorem is established for the orthogonal-array hypergraph when r=Theta(sqrt(n)).
No infinite family of orders is shown to satisfy a universal o(sqrt(n)) upper bound for f(n) or for compatible transversal resolutions.
No uniform construction of ITD(q+1,n;q) throughout the dyadic critical window q^2 <= n < 4q^2 is established.
No uniform critical-scale PBD theorem is established.
R0 is only an ambiguity diagnosis. It blocks adjudication but does not constitute a proof or disproof of the mathematical assertion.
R19 contains a malformed edge definition: e_x is written as {(i,j,x_i,x_j)<j}, so the hypergraph is not literally well-defined as displayed.

R8 uses unstated finite-dimensional linear-algebra results, including rank(A)=rank(A^T) and the implication that a trivial kernel of A^T gives full row rank, without stating their hypotheses or specifying the scalar field.
The candidate terminates with RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED rather than proving or disproving the exact statement, which directly violates the acceptance criteria.
The candidate's assertion UNCHECKED_IMPORTS: NONE is false because R8 relies on unstated linear-algebra facts and the post-R19 estimate has no identified derivation.
The computational falsification program required by S18 is not implemented or documented.
The exact immutable statement is not proved or disproved.

The expression n^2/binom(r,2)=Theta(n) after R19 is syntactically detached and unsupported. If intended as the size of a perfect matching, it is false: every perfect matching in the stated hypergraph has exactly n^2 edges.
The first-perturbation ITD construction is verified only for q=3 and q=4, not uniformly for prime powers.
The fragment following R19 appears to invoke an unidentified estimate producing n^2/binom(r,2) edges; no theorem, hypotheses, or derivation are supplied.
The notation ≫ is not defined in the immutable statement, and the supplied ambiguity rule forbids silently imposing the candidate's separately defined eventual absolute-constant interpretation.

The reported completeness score of 86 and adversarial-survival score of 96 materially overstate the submission because the interpretation gate, every terminal branch, and several intermediate subgoals remain unresolved.
